title: Todd Williams (singer)

infobox.value: is an Australian singer and songwriter from [Dubbo](/wiki/Dubbo "Dubbo"), New South Wales. He was the first indigenous winner of the [Star Maker](/w/index.php?title=Toyota_Star_Maker_Quest&action=edit&redlink=1 "Toyota Star Maker Quest (page does not exist)") at Tamworth[[1]](#cite_note-1) and won a [Deadly](/wiki/The_Deadlys "The Deadlys") in 2003 for Best Country Artist.[[2]](#cite_note-2) His first album, *Ten 'Till Midnight*, was recorded live[[3]](#cite_note-3) and released in 2004.
Tedmanson, Sophie (21 January 2003), "Star shines on Dubbo's karaoke kid", *[The Australian](/wiki/The_Australian "The Australian")*
Gilchrist, Inga (1 July 2004), "Got plenty in reserve", *MX (Australia)*
Maley, Jacqueline (2 July 2004), "Good news week", *[Sydney Morning Herald](/wiki/Sydney_Morning_Herald "Sydney Morning Herald")*
Adsett, Ron (September 2004), ["*Ten 'Till Midnight* review"](http://www.capitalnews.com.au/editorial.asp?editorial_id=579&issue=58), *Country City Capital News*

first_paragraph: **Todd Williams** is an Australian singer and songwriter from [Dubbo](/wiki/Dubbo "Dubbo"), New South Wales. He was the first indigenous winner of the [Star Maker](/w/index.php?title=Toyota_Star_Maker_Quest&action=edit&redlink=1 "Toyota Star Maker Quest (page does not exist)") at Tamworth[[1]](#cite_note-1) and won a [Deadly](/wiki/The_Deadlys "The Deadlys") in 2003 for Best Country Artist.[[2]](#cite_note-2) His first album, *Ten 'Till Midnight*, was recorded live[[3]](#cite_note-3) and released in 2004.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

| Todd Williams | |
| --- | --- |
| Genres | [Country](/wiki/Country_music "Country music") |
| Instrument | [Vocals](/wiki/Singing "Singing") |
| Label | Secret Street |

Musical artist

**Todd Williams** is an Australian singer and songwriter from [Dubbo](/wiki/Dubbo "Dubbo"), New South Wales. He was the first indigenous winner of the [Star Maker](/w/index.php?title=Toyota_Star_Maker_Quest&action=edit&redlink=1 "Toyota Star Maker Quest (page does not exist)") at Tamworth[[1]](#cite_note-1) and won a [Deadly](/wiki/The_Deadlys "The Deadlys") in 2003 for Best Country Artist.[[2]](#cite_note-2) His first album, *Ten 'Till Midnight*, was recorded live[[3]](#cite_note-3) and released in 2004.

## Discography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Todd_Williams_(singer)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Discography")]

* *Ten 'till midnight* (2004) - Secret Street[[4]](#cite_note-4)
* "Falling Down" - ABC Country[[5]](#cite_note-5)
